impact_on_program.next: Prove H_seam from SP/BC first-match payment theorem; construct ledger marks into [t]×F_p or D×F_p for isolated residual supports
impact_on_program.reduction: H_seam + (E2 or E5 injection) => residual mass atom. H_seam turns mass into core count; injection is the remaining mark.

E2_E5_bridge.name: matching_free_plus_injection_closes_mass
proof: H_seam ⇒ |R|=N_can_prim (matching-free mass law).
Injection of R or of C_res into L ⇒ |R|≤|L|.
v14: |R|≤t·p and |R|≤n·p both fit under TARGET.
rowsharp: E2 form is marked-incidence into {0..t-1}×F_p.

H_seam.name: H_seam_ledger_residual_matching_free
proof: Conditional: apply matching-free mass law once H_seam is granted.

Justification sketch (not a proof): sp_shift_pair / bc_chart are named first-match deletions targeting constant-shift / chart pairs; if every multi-member top-seam pencil is assigned there, residual cannot contain both ends of a seam edge.
Gap: need a theorem that SP/BC payment removes multi-member core pencils from residual support (not only image-cell charges).
H_seam.statement:
Hypothesis H_seam: the ledger residual R(z) (after first-match deletion of generated_field, quotient_planted, sparse_pade_hankel, m1_window_shadow, rank_drop_pivot, bc_chart, sp_shift_pair, extension_slope — v1/rowsharp) is matching-free in G_z.
Under H_seam, |R(z)| = N_can_prim(z), and residual mass reduces to bounding residual can-cores (or injecting them / residual supports into size ≤ t·p or n·p).
H_seam.status_of_H_seam: OPEN (intended by ledger branch list; not proved)

do_not: Unmarked top-seam counting without core mark (hostile audit)
Full-fiber n·p at shallow w (v2 counterexample)
OPEN_LEDGER_RESIDUAL_INJECTION.falsifier: Ledger residual R(z) with a top-seam edge inside R (refutes H_seam), or |R(z)| > t·p after all named first-match deletions (refutes E2 mass).
OPEN_LEDGER_RESIDUAL_INJECTION.name: OPEN_LEDGER_RESIDUAL_INJECTION
OPEN_LEDGER_RESIDUAL_INJECTION.statement:
Prove H_seam for ledger residual, AND prove one of:
  (i)  residual supports/cores inject into {0,...,t-1}×F_p (E2),
  (ii) residual supports/cores inject into D×F_p (E5),
using first-match marks (generated-field style row index, marked incidence with selector, full-rank pivot certificate, …), not aperiodic proxy alone.
Either closes residual mass at deployed row (v14/v15 bridges).

marked_incidence.name: top_seam_marked_incidence_normal_form
proof: Degree bound at e=w+1 forces A−B constant (v2 core pencil / rowsharp).
With G marked and orientation chosen, the free data is (B,c).
Hostile audit: without mark/selector this is not a global support cap.
marked_incidence.statement: For a top-seam pair with common core G marked, and sides A,B monic of degree e=w+1 with A−B=c ∈ F_p^×, the oriented seam neighbor injects into ordered split-translate data (B,c) (with divisor conditions B|Λ_E, B+c|Λ_{D\\E} up to orientation). This is a fixed-mark / pair normal form (rowsharp top-seam marked incidence; LQ top-seam audit), not a raw unmarked support bound.

matching_free_mass.consequence: Under matching-free residual, residual mass reduces exactly to the residual can-core count. Atom ⇔ N_can_prim ≤ TARGET (or ≤ t·p / n·p).
matching_free_mass.name: matching_free_residual_mass_law
proof: If two residual supports share C_can=C, their lex sides both lie in U(C,z) (same z, same core, both in fiber), hence form a top-seam edge, contradicting matching-free unless the supports are equal.
Thus C_can is injective on R, so |R| = N_can_prim(R).
Covering |R| ≤ pack · N_can with pack=1 is immediate.
matching_free_mass.statement: Let R ⊆ Fib_w(z) be matching-free in the top-seam graph G_z (no edge has both ends in R). Then for every core C, |{S ∈ R : C_can(S)=C}| ≤ 1. Consequently |R| = N_can_prim(R) := |{C_can(S): S∈R}|, and the residual lex covering improves from pack_ceil=17 to pack=1: |R| ≤ 1 · N_can_prim(R).

naive_marks.name: naive_isolated_marks_not_E2
proof: Computational certificates in toy_suite.
naive_marks.statement: On aperiodic + seam-free residual-proxy fibers, |R|=N_can, but maps S ↦ (min S, c_U), S ↦ (min U, c_U), S ↦ (min C, b_1) are not injective (per-fiber max label fiber ≥2 on multiple toys). These marks do not prove E2/E5.

oriented_first_mate.name: oriented_first_mate_on_nonisolated
oriented_first_mate.note: Under matching-free residual, R has no non-isolated residual vertices, so this injection is vacuous on residual — residual labels must come from isolated-support marks.
proof: Imported from v2 oriented first-mate injectivity.
oriented_first_mate.statement: Within Fib_w(z), non-isolated vertices (degree ≥1 in G_z) inject into oriented first-mate edge keys (C, high_side_coeffs, min(c_U,c_V), max(c_U,c_V), side) (v2 Thm 3). This is structural bookkeeping for seam pairs; the label space is not of size t·p or n·p.

side_determined.name: matching_free_side_determined_by_core_and_z
proof: Matching-free ⇒ at most one residual support per core.
That support is C ∪ U for the unique residual side U ∈ U(C,z) ∩ R.
v3 triangular inversion: b = b(z,u) couples core and side prefixes.
side_determined.statement: Under matching-free R ⊆ Fib_w(z), each residual S is uniquely determined by C=C_can(S): the side U is the unique member of U(C,z) that appears in R (at most one), and triangular inversion relates side prefix u to core prefix b and z (v3).

top_seam_graph.name: top_seam_graph_core_pencil
proof: v2 Theorem 1: sides for fixed (C,z) form a CS pencil packing into D\\C.
v3: C_can is the lex core; top-seam at e=w+1 is exactly free-1 CS.
Two supports with the same can-core and sides in U(C,z) are adjacent.
top_seam_graph.statement: Fix z. On vertex set Fib_w(z), declare an edge between distinct S, S' when C_can(S)=C_can(S')=C and the lex sides U, U' both lie in the core pencil U(C,z). By the core-pencil theorem (v2), U(C,z) is empty or a free-1 constant-shift family, so the connected components of this graph inside each core are cliques of size |U(C,z)| ≤ floor((n-|C|)/e) = pack_ceil (deployed 17).

title: Ledger residual injection: top-seam matching-free + E2/E5 bridges
